frameworks.name: limited resource model of visual working memory
frameworks.one_line: Memory capacity is a continuous resource shared dynamically across items

frameworks.name: fixed-slot (item-limit) model
frameworks.one_line: Capacity as a discrete fixed number (~4) of stored objects

frameworks.name: selective attention allocation
frameworks.one_line: Attention biases distribution of memory resource across scene items

core_question: Is visual working memory limited by a fixed number of items or by a flexibly shared resource?
key_assumptions: Recall precision indexes the amount of resource allocated to an item
Resource allocation can be measured per-item in a multi-item scene
Eye-movement targets and attended items receive prioritized allocation
manipulations: Varied number of items in the visual scene
Biased allocation via selective attention cues
Biased allocation via targets of upcoming eye movements
key_findings: Memory precision decreases as more items are added rather than capacity hitting a hard 4-item cap
Attention shifts more resource (and thus higher precision) to cued items
Saccade targets receive elevated memory resource allocation
Precision relates to allocated proportion through a simple power law
interpretations: Visual working memory is a dynamically shared limited resource, not a fixed slot count
Resource allocation is flexibly governed by attention and oculomotor priority
